---
title: "SPEC-QUESTION-SETS-0.0.2.qmd"
---

# Question Sets — SPEC 0.0.2

This spec defines **question sets**: named, ordered lists of questions that can be used as a test, a homework assignment, or a study list. It extends the MVP (SPEC-0.0.1) by adding UI and persistence for creating and managing question sets, and clarifies terminology so that "test" is reserved for the *activity* of taking and submitting answers, not for the underlying set of questions.

**References:** [SPEC-0.0.1.qmd](SPEC-0.0.1.qmd), [PRD-0.0.1-v003.qmd](PRD-0.0.1-v003.qmd), [QUESTION-FORMAT-0.0.1.qmd](QUESTION-FORMAT-0.0.1.qmd), [QUESTION-MODULE-SPEC.qmd](QUESTION-MODULE-SPEC.qmd), [QUESTION-SET-FORMAT-0.0.2.qmd](QUESTION-SET-FORMAT-0.0.2.qmd).  
See [DOC-GUIDE-v001.qmd](DOC-GUIDE-v001.qmd) for document versioning.

---

## Purpose and scope

### What is a question set?

A **question set** is a named, ordered list of question identifiers. The same question set can be used in different ways:

- **As a test** — A learner takes the set under timed/untimed conditions, submits answers, and receives a score. This is the "take as test" flow.
- **As homework** — The same set of questions might be assigned as homework (same or different submission rules in a future version).
- **As a study list** — A topic-focused set of questions for practice, with or without grading.

The system does **not** assume every question set is a "test." Naming and UI should reflect that question sets are generic; the *use* of a set (e.g. "Take as test") is a separate action.

### Naming: "Question set" vs "Test"

| Term | Meaning in this spec |
|------|----------------------|
| **Question set** | A named set of questions (data). Can be used as a test, homework, or study list. |
| **Test** | The *activity* of taking a question set: start session, answer questions, submit, get score. "Test" = one *usage* of a question set. |
| **Test session** | One user attempt (e.g. one "take" of a question set). Stored in DB as `test_sessions`. |

So: **question sets** are the reusable data; **tests** (and test sessions) are the act of taking and submitting. The UI and docs should avoid implying that every question set is "a test"; prefer "question set" for the entity and "Take as test" (or similar) for the action.

### Scope for 0.0.2

- **In scope**
  - Persist question sets in the database (new tables and APIs).
  - UI to **create** a new question set: choose a name (and optional description) and **select which questions** to include (from available questions).
  - UI to **list** question sets; from the list, the user can take a set as a test (take flow) or later edit/delete (optional for 0.0.2).
  - Remove the config-based "tests" (`tests-config.ts`, `TESTS_CONFIG` env). Question sets are stored in the **database** (source of truth). The **filesystem** under `question-sets/` is imported via **sync** only (same model as questions). See [QUESTION-SET-FORMAT-0.0.2.qmd](QUESTION-SET-FORMAT-0.0.2.qmd) for the file layout; sync upserts into `question_sets` with `source_slug` set to the folder name.
  - Update documentation and code naming so that "question set" is used for the set of questions and "test" is used only for the taking/submission flow.

- **Out of scope for 0.0.2**
  - Editing or deleting question sets (can be added later).
  - Different "modes" per set (e.g. homework vs test); for now, every question set can be "taken as a test."
  - Ordering of questions within a set (fixed order or shuffle) is MVP-defined; spec allows storing order.

---

## Data model

### New table: `question_sets`

Store one row per question set.

| Column | Type | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `id` | uuid, PK | Unique question set id (e.g. `gen_random_uuid()`). |
| `title` | text, not null | User-facing name (e.g. "Bash basics", "Week 3 homework"). |
| `description` | text, nullable | Optional description. |
| `owner_id` | uuid, nullable, FK → auth.users | User who created the set. Nullable to allow system/seed data. |
| `created_at` | timestamptz | When the set was created. |
| `source_slug` | text, nullable, unique when present | Folder name under `question-sets/` when the set was synced from the filesystem; null for UI-created sets. |

### New table: `question_set_items`

Store the questions in each question set with explicit order.

| Column | Type | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `id` | uuid, PK | Unique row id. |
| `question_set_id` | uuid, not null, FK → question_sets(id) ON DELETE CASCADE | Which question set. |
| `question_logical_id` | text, not null | Question logical id (same as in question_versions / storage). |
| `position` | int, not null | 1-based order within the set. |

Unique constraint on `(question_set_id, question_logical_id)` so the same question appears at most once per set. Order is given by `position`.

### Sources of question set data

- **Database (source of truth)**: Tables `question_sets` and `question_set_items`. Listing, get-by-id, and take flow use the DB only. Optional column `source_slug` identifies sets imported from a repo folder.
- **Filesystem (import only)**: Per [QUESTION-SET-FORMAT-0.0.2.qmd](QUESTION-SET-FORMAT-0.0.2.qmd), one folder per set with `set.yaml`. **Sync** (startup and `POST /api/admin/sync-questions`) reads these folders and upserts into the DB—same approach as question sync. There is no runtime merge of DB + filesystem lists.

### Changes to existing tables

- **test_sessions**: Add column `set_id` (uuid, not null, FK → question_sets(id)). This is the question set used for this test attempt. Remove or stop using `test_id`; existing migrations may need a new migration that adds `set_id` and drops `test_id` (or a single new migration for a fresh install).
- **answers**: No change; they reference `session_id` and store `question_id` / `question_version`.

### RLS (row-level security)

- **question_sets**: Authenticated users can read all question sets (so everyone can "take as test" any set). Insert: authenticated users can create (set `owner_id` = self). Update/delete: only owner (or later, admin) when edit/delete is added.
- **question_set_items**: Read for authenticated; insert/update/delete only when the user owns the parent question set (policy checks `question_sets.owner_id`).

---

## API

### Question sets

| Method / path | Auth | Purpose |
|---------------|------|---------|
| `GET /api/question-sets` | Authenticated | List all question sets (id, title, description, question count). Optional: filter by owner. |
| `GET /api/question-sets/[id]` | Authenticated | Get one question set by id with ordered list of question logical ids (for "take as test" flow). |
| `POST /api/question-sets` | Authenticated | Create question set. Body: `{ title: string, description?: string, questionLogicalIds: string[] }`. Returns `{ id, title, ... }`. |

For 0.0.2, no PATCH/DELETE is required; they can be added in a later spec.

### Take-as-test flow

- **Start test**: `POST /api/question-sets/[id]/start` — `id` is the question set id (uuid). Server loads the question set, gets ordered `question_logical_id` list, creates a test_session with `set_id = id`, returns `sessionId`, `questionIds`, etc.
- **Submit test**: `POST /api/question-sets/[id]/submit` — `id` is the question set id. Body and behavior as today (sessionId, answers); server validates session and records results.

List of question sets for the learner is `GET /api/question-sets`. The "Take as test" action uses the question set id with the start and submit endpoints above.

---

## UI

### Home / navigation

- **Question sets**: Link to list of question sets. Primary entry for learners and creators.
- **Create question set**: From the list page, a button/link "Create question set" that goes to the create page.

### Question sets list page

- **Route**: `/question-sets`.
- **Content**: List all question sets (title, description, question count). Each row has an action "Take as test" that goes to the take flow (e.g. `/question-sets/[id]/take` where `id` is the question set uuid). "Create question set" button.

### Create question set page

- **Route**: `/question-sets/new`.
- **Content**:
  - **Title** (required): text input.
  - **Description** (optional): textarea.
  - **Questions**: Multi-select or checkbox list of **available questions**. Questions are loaded from `GET /api/questions` or `GET /api/questions/my` (or a dedicated "list all questions for picker" endpoint). User selects which questions to include; order = order in the list when submitted (or a separate ordering step in a later version).
  - **Submit**: POST to `POST /api/question-sets` with `title`, `description`, `questionLogicalIds`; then redirect to question sets list or to the new set's "Take as test" page.

No edit/delete UI required for 0.0.2.

### Take flow

- **Route**: `/question-sets/[id]/take` — `id` = question set id.
- **Behavior**: Start session via `POST /api/question-sets/[id]/start`, load questions by logical id, answer, submit via `POST /api/question-sets/[id]/submit`. Same flow as today; only the source of question ids (DB question set) and the URL/API paths change.

### Naming in the UI

- Use **"Question sets"** for the list and **"Create question set"** for the creation flow.
- Use **"Take as test"** (or "Take test") for the action that starts a test session from a question set.

---

## Code and config changes

### New and changed code

- **Migrations**: New migration adding `question_sets` and `question_set_items` with RLS. Migration for `test_sessions`: add `set_id` (FK to question_sets), remove `test_id` (or create `test_sessions` with `set_id` from the start if the project can do a breaking schema change).
- **Config**: Remove `src/lib/tests-config.ts` and env `TESTS_CONFIG`. Question sets come from the DB and/or from the filesystem per QUESTION-SET-FORMAT-0.0.2; implement a loader that reads from the configured source(s). Seed one default question set (DB or file) for development if needed.
- **API routes**: Add `src/app/api/question-sets/route.ts` (GET list, POST create) and `src/app/api/question-sets/[id]/route.ts` (GET one with question ids). Add `src/app/api/question-sets/[id]/start/route.ts` and `src/app/api/question-sets/[id]/submit/route.ts` (move and adapt logic from the current tests API; use `set_id` in test_sessions). Remove or deprecate `src/app/api/tests/` routes.
- **Lib**: e.g. `src/lib/question-sets.ts` (or separate `question-sets-db.ts` and `question-sets-fs.ts`) for helpers that load question sets from the DB and/or from the filesystem per QUESTION-SET-FORMAT-0.0.2.
- **Pages**: `src/app/question-sets/page.tsx` (list), `src/app/question-sets/new/page.tsx` (create), `src/app/question-sets/[id]/take/page.tsx` (take as test). Remove or redirect old `src/app/tests/` routes to the new question-set routes.

### Naming and documentation

- **Code**: Use `questionSet`, `questionSetId`, `QuestionSet`, `QuestionSetItem`. Use `set_id` in test_sessions. No `TestDef` for the set itself; use `QuestionSet` (or equivalent) for the type that holds id, title, description, question ids.
- **Docs**: Update SPEC-0.0.1 (or add a short note) that "tests" in the student sense are backed by question sets. Update DEVELOPER-GUIDE, README, and any doc that refers to "create tests" or "tests config" to "question sets" and the new API/routes. Update QUESTION-MODULE-SPEC section "Tests and test flow" to say that tests are taken from question sets and that `set_id` in test_sessions references `question_sets.id`.

---

## Summary of terminology (for implementation and docs)

| Concept | Use in UI/code/docs |
|--------|----------------------|
| The set of questions | **Question set** |
| Creating a set of questions | **Create question set**; user **chooses questions** to add |
| The activity of answering and submitting | **Take (as) test**; **test session** |
| DB table for the set | `question_sets` + `question_set_items` |
| DB table for the attempt | `test_sessions` with `set_id` → `question_sets.id` |

This keeps the codebase and docs clear: **question sets** are the reusable data (tests, homework, study); **tests** are the timed/untimed take-and-submit experience backed by a question set.

---

## Open items / TODOs (for implementation phase)

- [ ] Add migration for `question_sets` and `question_set_items` and RLS.
- [ ] Add migration for `test_sessions`: add `set_id`, remove `test_id`.
- [ ] Implement GET/POST `/api/question-sets` and GET `/api/question-sets/[id]`.
- [ ] Implement `POST /api/question-sets/[id]/start` and `POST /api/question-sets/[id]/submit`.
- [ ] Implement question sets list page and create page (with question picker).
- [ ] Implement take flow at `/question-sets/[id]/take`.
- [ ] Remove `tests-config.ts` and `/api/tests/`, `/tests/` routes (or redirect to question-sets).
- [ ] Update all user-facing strings and docs to use "question set" and "Take as test" consistently.
- [ ] Support loading question sets from filesystem per QUESTION-SET-FORMAT-0.0.2 (and optionally merge with DB).
- [ ] Optional: seed a default question set (file or DB) for development.

---

## Related documents

- **SPEC-0.0.1.qmd** — MVP scope; tests and question storage.
- **PRD-0.0.1-v003.qmd** — Product vision; database schema (align with question sets).
- **QUESTION-FORMAT-0.0.1.qmd** — Question layout and metadata.
- **QUESTION-SET-FORMAT-0.0.2.qmd** — File-based question set layout and `set.yaml` schema.
- **QUESTION-MODULE-SPEC.qmd** — Question API and "Tests and test flow" section to update.
- **DOC-GUIDE-v001.qmd** — Document versioning.
